dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: The assembled bundle does not meet the spec: `runForStep` walks `Defaults -> Artifact -> TargetFilter` before `strategy.Apply` adds skills/references/learnings (`internal/cli/context/cmd.go:192-203`), so target filtering is never applied to the final bundle; in a layer-tagged sandbox, `--target operator`, `driver`, and `specialist:go-specialist` all returned the same unfiltered skill set and no specialist brief.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: The new tests miss the main failure path: `tests/integration/test-context-routing.sh:117-139` treats a `skill_layer_unset` blocker as success instead of validating a real bundle, so there is no test covering successful `runForStep` assembly, target filtering, or specialist-brief inclusion where the ordering bug lives.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: Several contract points differ from the spec: the chain is only `DefaultsNode -> ArtifactNode -> TargetFilterNode` (`internal/cli/context/chain.go:206-215`) instead of `defaults -> step strategy -> row overrides -> target filter`; `step_strategy_metadata` is left open without `additionalProperties:false` (`schemas/context-bundle.schema.json:148-150`); and `ListSkills` only scans top-level `skills/*.md` (`internal/cli/context/source.go:175-201`), omitting `skills/shared/*` and never injecting `specialists/{id}.md`.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: The touched files are confined to the context CLI surface, schema, fixture, integration test, `commands/work.md.tmpl`, and `internal/cli/app.go`, and those additions are explicitly documented in the commit message as part of the deliverable.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: The snapshot is gofmt-clean and `go test ./...` passes in the exported D4 tree, with project conventions such as wrapped errors and colocated tests generally followed despite the functional/spec gaps.
